Finnhub News.headline: US NAHB April housing market index 34 vs 37 expected
Finnhub News.summary:
• Prior was 38
  • Single family vs 42 prior
  • Prospective buyers vs 25 prior
  • Sales expectations vs 49 prior

The US housing industry is in a deep recession and rising rates following the Iran war aren't going to help.

The NAHB/Wells Fargo Housing Market Index (HMI), published monthly by the National Association of Home Builders, is one of the most widely followed gauges of sen

source_metadata.headline: US NAHB April housing market index 34 vs 37 expected
source_metadata.summary:
• Prior was 38
  • Single family vs 42 prior
  • Prospective buyers vs 25 prior
  • Sales expectations vs 49 prior

The US housing industry is in a deep recession and rising rates following the Iran war aren't going to help.

The NAHB/Wells Fargo Housing Market Index (HMI), published monthly by the National Association of Home Builders, is one of the most widely followed gauges of sentiment in the U.S. residential construction industry. Derived from a survey that NAHB has conducted for more than 40 years, the index polls roughly 900 home builders nationwide, asking them to rate current single-family home sales as good, fair, or poor, to assess expected sales over the next six months, and to evaluate the traffic of prospective buyers. Scores from these three components are combined into a seasonally adjusted composite index, where 50 is the dividing line — readings above 50 indicate that more builders view conditions favorably, while readings below signal a negative outlook.

The HMI has remained stubbornly below 50 throughout 2025 and into 2026, reflecting persistent affordability headwinds from elevated mortgage rates, high construction costs, and stretched home prices relative to incomes. The index closed 2025 at 39 in December, then slid to 37 in January 2026 as all three subcomponents declined. February brought a further dip to 36, a five-month low, with future sales expectations falling to 44 — well below the breakeven threshold. March saw a modest rebound to 38, with all three components ticking higher: current sales rose to 42, expected sales improved to 49, and buyer traffic edged up to 25.

Throughout this period, roughly two-thirds of builders have reported offering sales incentives, and over a third have been cutting prices — averaging around 6% — underscoring the degree to which affordability constraints continue to weigh on demand despite lower mortgage rates relative to a year ago.

This article was written by Adam Button at investinglive.com.

BoC.headline: Release: Business Outlook Survey and Canadian Survey of Consumer Expectations
BoC.summary:
11:30 (ET)
The Business Outlook Survey is a summary of interviews conducted by the Bank's regional offices with business leaders from about 100 firms, selected in accordance with the composition of Canada's gross domestic product. The Canadian Survey of Consumer Expectations is a quarterly survey aimed at measuring household views of inflation, the labour market and household finances, as well as topical issues of interest to the Bank of Canada.

BoC.headline: Interest Rate Announcement and Monetary Policy Report
BoC.summary: 09:45 (ET)On eight scheduled dates each year, the Bank of Canada announces the setting for the overnight rate target in a press release explaining the factors behind the decision. Four times a year, Governing Council presents the Monetary Policy Report: the Bank’s base-case projection for inflation and growth in the Canadian economy, and its assessment of risks.

relevance_assessments.relevance_reasoning: The Market Participants Survey provides insights into market expectations and sentiment, which can influence the value of the Canadian dollar against the US dollar.
relevance_assessments.headline: Release: Market Participants Survey
relevance_assessments.summary:
10:30 (ET)
The Market Participants Survey is conducted quarterly. Bank of Canada staff reach out to a diverse set of participants in financial markets to gather their views on key macroeconomic and financial variables as well as on monetary policy.

relevance_assessments.relevance_reasoning: The Interest Rate Announcement and Monetary Policy Report by the Bank of Canada can significantly impact the value of the Canadian dollar against the US dollar, as changes in interest rates can influence currency valuations.
relevance_assessments.headline: Interest Rate Announcement and Monetary Policy Report
relevance_assessments.summary: 09:45 (ET)On eight scheduled dates each year, the Bank of Canada announces the setting for the overnight rate target in a press release explaining the factors behind the decision. Four times a year, Governing Council presents the Monetary Policy Report: the Bank’s base-case projection for inflation and growth in the Canadian economy, and its assessment of risks.

relevance_assessments.relevance_reasoning: The Business Outlook Survey and Canadian Survey of Consumer Expectations are important indicators of the Canadian economy, which can impact the value of the Canadian dollar against the US dollar.
relevance_assessments.headline: Release: Business Outlook Survey and Canadian Survey of Consumer Expectations
relevance_assessments.summary:
11:30 (ET)
The Business Outlook Survey is a summary of interviews conducted by the Bank's regional offices with business leaders from about 100 firms, selected in accordance with the composition of Canada's gross domestic product. The Canadian Survey of Consumer Expectations is a quarterly survey aimed at measuring household views of inflation, the labour market and household finances, as well as topical issues of interest to the Bank of Canada.

signals.reasoning: The recent news headlines regarding national holidays (Thanksgiving Day, Remembrance Day, Christmas Day, and Boxing Day) may lead to reduced trading volumes and volatility in the forex market. However, these holidays do not provide a clear directional bias for XAU/USD. The current bullish trend may continue, but the reduced liquidity and volatility during holidays could lead to unpredictable price movements. The evidence is weak, as it is based on general market conditions during holidays rather than specific news about XAU/USD or its underlying factors.
key_factors: Lack of specific news about XAU/USD
risk_factors: Unpredictable price movements due to low liquidity
Potential for surprise news or events during holidays
Continuation of the bullish trend despite holiday season

signals.reasoning: The recent news and market context suggest a bearish trend for USD/CHF. The Swiss National Bank's expected annual profit, adjustment of remuneration of sight deposits, establishment of the Extended Liquidity Facility, and Monetary Policy Assessment can impact the Swiss economy and influence the value of the Swiss franc against the US dollar. The current price and 24h change also indicate a bearish trend. However, the national holidays in the US and Canada may lead to reduced trading volumes and volatility, which could affect the currency pair. The evidence is not extremely strong, but multiple sources from the Swiss National Bank suggest a potential strengthening of the Swiss franc.
key_factors: Swiss National Bank's expected annual profit
Adjustment of remuneration of sight deposits
Establishment of the Extended Liquidity Facility
risk_factors: National holidays in the US and Canada leading to reduced trading volumes and volatility
Unexpected changes in US economic policy or data releases
Global economic events that could impact the Swiss franc or US dollar

challenge_output.counter_argument: The proposed trade is based on expected actions by the Swiss National Bank, but it does not consider the potential for the US Federal Reserve to take countermeasures that could strengthen the US dollar, thereby offsetting any gains from a strengthening Swiss franc.
challenge_output.alternative_interpretation: The Swiss National Bank's actions could be seen as a sign of economic stability, which might attract foreign investment and actually weaken the Swiss franc against the US dollar, as investors become more confident in the Swiss economy and less likely to seek safe-haven assets.

signals.reasoning: The recent news and market context suggest that the Canadian dollar may strengthen against the US dollar. The Business Outlook Survey and Canadian Survey of Consumer Expectations may indicate a positive outlook for the Canadian economy, which could lead to an increase in the value of the Canadian dollar. Additionally, the Interest Rate Announcement and Monetary Policy Report by the Bank of Canada may lead to changes in interest rates, which could also impact the value of the Canadian dollar. The recent trend is bullish, but the upcoming national holidays may lead to reduced trading volumes and volatility. Overall, the evidence suggests a potential short position on USD/CAD.
risk_factors: Unexpected changes in interest rates
Surprising results from the Business Outlook Survey and Canadian Survey of Consumer Expectations
Reduced trading volumes and volatility due to national holidays
Unforeseen global economic events

challenge_output.counter_argument: The recent trend is bullish, but it may be overextended, and the reduced trading volumes and volatility due to national holidays could lead to a short squeeze, causing the USD/CAD to rise instead of fall.
challenge_output.alternative_interpretation: The Business Outlook Survey and Canadian Survey of Consumer Expectations may indicate a positive outlook for the Canadian economy, but they could also lead to increased expectations of interest rate hikes, which would strengthen the US dollar against the Canadian dollar.
